seo.title: 10 Best Hotels in Chiang Khong 2026 — Riverside Guesthouses & Mekong Views
seo.metaDescription: Find the best hotels and guesthouses in Chiang Khong for every budget. From riverside budget rooms to the town's best mid-range hotels. Real prices, honest reviews, and Mekong views.

intro: Chiang Khong's accommodation is almost entirely concentrated along Sai Klang Road, the main riverside street facing the Mekong and Laos beyond. Despite the town's small size, the range is surprisingly good — from bare-bones fan rooms at 250 THB to comfortable mid-range riverside resorts with pools. The vast majority of visitors spend one night; those who stay longer tend to upgrade to a place with a riverside terrace and order another plate of pla buek. Here are 10 properties covering every budget — all are real hotels with real prices.

hotels.name: Portside Hotel
hotels.description: One of Chiang Khong's most characterful budget options, the Portside sits directly on the Mekong riverbank with a flat roof that doubles as a sunset terrace. Clean, basic rooms with air conditioning and en-suite bathrooms face the river. Run by a friendly local owner, it has the lived-in warmth of a proper guesthouse rather than a corporate hotel. Popular with overland travelers on the Southeast Asia trail.
highlights: Direct Mekong riverfront with rooftop terrace
Clean air-conditioned rooms with en-suite at budget prices
Friendly owner with local knowledge about the border crossing and slow boat

hotels.name: Baanfai Guesthouse Chiangkhong
hotels.description: A well-established and highly rated guesthouse with its main entrance on Sai Klang Road and buildings extending back to the river with terrace views. Fan rooms are simple with bathrooms and hot showers; air-conditioned rooms are spacious with good balconies. There is a coffee shop on-site adjacent to a Thai Lu and hilltribe textile souvenir shop. Guest reviews consistently praise the friendly owners, cleanliness, and prime central location.
highlights: Central location on Sai Klang Road with riverside buildings
On-site coffee shop and textile souvenir shop
Mix of fan and AC rooms to suit different budgets

hotels.name: Baanrimtaling Home Stay
hotels.description: A charming riverside establishment built from wood with a pleasant restaurant area possessing excellent Mekong views. The rooms are simply furnished but clean, and the wooden construction gives the guesthouse genuine local character. The restaurant emphasizes vegetarian dishes alongside standard Thai fare — a rarity in Chiang Khong and appreciated by many travelers. A quiet, authentic choice away from the main tourist cluster.
highlights: Wooden construction with genuine local character
Riverside restaurant with vegetarian-friendly menu
Quieter location away from the main guesthouse strip

hotels.name: Namkhong Guesthouse and Resort
hotels.description: One of Chiang Khong's best-known guesthouses and a rarity for the budget category — it has a swimming pool. Rooms range from basic fan rooms to air-conditioned units with river views. The Mekong-facing grounds are pleasant for a morning coffee while watching Laotian fishermen on the water. A reliable, well-managed property that handles both budget backpackers and more comfort-seeking travelers.
highlights: Swimming pool — uncommon at this price range in Chiang Khong
Range of room types from fan to air-con with river views
Well-established with consistent management and cleanliness

hotels.name: Day Waterfront Hotel
hotels.description: Chiang Khong's best-rated hotel with a score of 9.1 from nearly 1,000 reviews. The Day Waterfront offers super-spacious, modern rooms with all balconies facing the Mekong River and Laos beyond. The design is clean and contemporary — a significant step up from the town's guesthouses in terms of room quality and facilities, while remaining well-priced for the standard. Close to the border crossing, making it ideal for early morning departures.
highlights: All rooms have river-facing balconies with direct Mekong views
Spacious, modern rooms at competitive mid-range prices
Excellent location near the border bridge for easy morning crossings

hotels.name: Namkhong Riverside Hotel
hotels.description: A purpose-built riverside hotel right on the Mekong bank in the town center. The design is clever — a guaranteed Mekong view from every room, with better views as you ascend the hotel's five floors. Each room has its own small private balcony, and the top-floor rooms offer panoramic views over the river to Laos. A good middle ground between the guesthouses and the resort-style properties.
highlights: Guaranteed Mekong view from every room with private balcony
Top-floor rooms offer panoramic views to Laos
Central location steps from the best riverside cafes and restaurants

hotels.name: Fortune Riverview Hotel Chiang Khong
hotels.description: The largest hotel in Chiang Khong with 147 fully equipped rooms, all with balconies and flat-screen TVs. Facilities include an outdoor swimming pool, an all-day dining restaurant, and function rooms. A step above the town's guesthouses in terms of formal hotel facilities, and good value compared to similar-standard properties in Chiang Rai city. The outdoor pool is a genuine bonus on hot days.
highlights: Largest hotel in town with 147 rooms and full facilities
Outdoor swimming pool and all-day dining restaurant
All rooms have balconies — upper floors have river views

hotels.name: Siam Tara Resort Chiangkhong
hotels.description: A well-regarded resort property with 40 air-conditioned accommodations, a pool, and an on-site restaurant. Guest reviews highlight the beautiful pool, wonderful views of Laos across the Mekong, and excellently presented meals. A quieter, more resort-like atmosphere than the town-center guesthouses. Good for travelers who want to unwind for a few days rather than simply transit.
highlights: Beautiful pool with Mekong and Laos views
On-site restaurant with well-presented local cuisine
Resort atmosphere in a small town setting

hotels.name: Chiang Khong Hill Resort
hotels.description: Set on the hillside above the town rather than on the riverbank, the Chiang Khong Hill Resort offers a different perspective — panoramic views over the town, the Mekong River, and Laos from elevated terraces. Rooms are comfortable and clean with air conditioning. The hill setting gives some separation from the town noise and the elevated views of the Mekong valley are genuinely impressive, particularly at sunrise.
highlights: Hillside panoramic views over Chiang Khong, the Mekong, and Laos
Quieter than riverside properties — good for light sleepers
Panoramic sunrise views over the Mekong valley

hotels.name: Golden Triangle Inn Chiang Khong
hotels.description: One of Chiang Khong's more polished mid-range options, offering comfortable rooms with modern amenities and a riverside location. A swimming pool, on-site restaurant serving Thai and Lanna cuisine, and attentive service make it popular with travelers who want hotel-standard quality in a town of mostly guesthouses. Well-positioned for the border crossing, the Friday walking street, and riverside dining.
highlights: Hotel-standard facilities in a guesthouse town
Pool and on-site restaurant with Lanna cuisine
Knowledgeable staff to assist with Laos border and slow boat logistics

bookingTips: Book at least one night before your Laos slow boat crossing — the 9 AM departure from Huay Xai makes same-day arrival from Chiang Rai a gamble
Riverside rooms on Sai Klang Road are worth the small premium — a Mekong-facing balcony at sunset is one of the defining Chiang Khong experiences
Most guesthouses fill up on Thursday and Friday nights when the Friday walking street attracts visitors from Chiang Rai — book ahead for weekend stays
Accommodation in Chiang Khong is significantly cheaper than Chiang Rai city — budget 300-600 THB for a clean fan room, 800-1,500 THB for mid-range with AC and pool
Ask your guesthouse about slow boat logistics — most owners know the current departure times, visa requirements, and bridge bus schedules
In low season (May-October) prices drop 20-40% and walk-in availability is good — but still book the night before a slow boat departure
